title: Call of Duty® 2

notes: With the latest drivers and Proton version after disabling Esync the game runs flawless. Performance is solid even at very high resolutions and I didn't encounter any stability issues.
title: Call of Duty® 2

notes: Works great out the box, did not test singleplayer. Performance is about the same as windows. Helpfull console commands:  /cg_drawfps 1 (show fps counter) /com_maxfps 250 (set fps cap) /cg_fov 80 (set fov)
title: Call of Duty® 2

notes: after disable esync the game start and run fine (single player)
title: Call of Duty® 2

notes: Started up with the multiplayer, applied ingame settings such as Resolution, graphics etc (1920x1080 resolution with highest settings) and ran cg_drawfps 1 to show FPS. FPS was about 90 FPS after com_maxfps was removed (only limits the FPS by engine default). Ran a few maps and they were all fine, not dropping below 60 fps. Microstutters but that could be my laptop. Decided to switch from MP to SP, the MP process was still active in the background so it doesnt quit the MP instance when you select SP as is the case with Windows (It probably is the same vice versa). Single Player runs fine also at the same settings without tweaks.
title: Call of Duty® 2

notes: Clean install. However, as others had said, movies are black (you can hear them though). This includes the opening company logos. Was able to get to menus, but the game shows black when you get ingame (never saw anything). Totally borked on my end.
